text.title: Six Core Principles (PMBOK 7) Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the six-core-principles methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the final rule defines when to skip this methodology entirely.

rule.statement: Every principle audit score MUST cite at least one observable artefact (decision log entry, retro line, satisfaction score).
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: 'Focus on Value' MUST show at least one decision where scope was cut to protect outcome value.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: 'Embed Quality' MUST point to a quality gate that ran and failed at least once (i.e., is real, not theatre).
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: 'Lead Accountably' MUST name a single accountable owner per major decision (no consensus blur).
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: 'Build Empowered Teams' MUST show ≥3 decisions in 90 days made by the team without escalation.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Skip when PMBOK 7 is not the governing standard or audit cost > benefit.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.
